title: Total Delivered Revenue by Seller
problem_statement: Calculate the total revenue for each seller considering only delivered orders. Return seller_id, seller name, and total_revenue sorted by revenue descending.
hint: JOIN orders and sellers, filter status='delivered', GROUP BY seller.
solution_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, s.name AS seller_name, SUM(o.amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN sellers s ON o.seller_id = s.seller_id WHERE o.status = 'delivered' GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

title: Top Product by Region
problem_statement: Find the top-selling product by total quantity in each seller region (delivered orders only). Return region, product_name, total_qty.
hint: Aggregate then use ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_qty DESC).
explanation: Top-N per group using window functions.
solution_sql: WITH ps AS (SELECT s.region, p.name AS product_name, SUM(o.quantity) AS total_qty FROM orders o JOIN sellers s ON o.seller_id=s.seller_id JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.product_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY s.region, p.name), rk AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_qty DESC) AS rn FROM ps) SELECT region, product_name, total_qty FROM rk WHERE rn=1 ORDER BY region

title: Repeat Customers
problem_statement: Find customers with more than 2 distinct orders. Return customer_id, name, order_count desc.
hint: GROUP BY customer_id, HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) > 2.
solution_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) > 2 ORDER BY order_count DESC

title: Customers With No Delivered Orders
problem_statement: Find customers who have never had a delivered order. Return customer_id and name.
hint: LEFT JOIN with status filter, or NOT EXISTS / LEFT ANTI join.
explanation: Anti-join pattern for finding missing matches.
solution_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id=o.customer_id AND o.status='delivered' WHERE o.order_id IS NULL ORDER BY c.customer_id

title: Latest Order Per Customer
problem_statement: For each customer find their most recent order. Return customer_id, name, order_id, order_date, amount.
hint: ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date DESC).
explanation: Latest record per key — essential deduplication pattern.
solution_sql: WITH rk AS (SELECT o.*, c.name, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY o.customer_id ORDER BY o.order_date DESC) AS rn FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id) SELECT customer_id, name, order_id, order_date, amount FROM rk WHERE rn=1 ORDER BY customer_id

title: Duplicate Order Detection
problem_statement: Find duplicate order_ids in the orders table. Return order_id and occurrence_count.
hint: GROUP BY order_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1.
explanation: Duplicate detection — critical for data quality.
solution_sql: SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS occurrence_count FROM orders GROUP BY order_id HAVING COUNT(*)>1

title: Seller Target vs Actual Sales
problem_statement: Compare seller target_revenue vs actual delivered revenue. Return seller_id, name, target_revenue, actual_revenue, met_target boolean.
hint: LEFT JOIN sellers to aggregated orders, COALESCE for nulls.
explanation: KPI comparison pattern common in reporting.
solution_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, s.name, s.target_revenue, COALESCE(SUM(o.amount),0) AS actual_revenue, CASE WHEN COALESCE(SUM(o.amount),0)>=s.target_revenue THEN true ELSE false END AS met_target FROM sellers s LEFT JOIN orders o ON s.seller_id=o.seller_id AND o.status='delivered' GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.name, s.target_revenue ORDER BY s.seller_id

title: Products With No Sales
problem_statement: Find products never ordered. Return product_id and name.
hint: LEFT JOIN products to orders, WHERE order_id IS NULL.
solution_sql: SELECT p.product_id, p.name FROM products p LEFT JOIN orders o ON p.product_id=o.product_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL ORDER BY p.product_id

title: Daily GMV Trend
problem_statement: Calculate daily GMV (total amount all statuses) per order_date, sorted by date.
hint: GROUP BY order_date, SUM(amount).
solution_sql: SELECT order_date, SUM(amount) AS daily_gmv FROM orders GROUP BY order_date ORDER BY order_date

title: Running Total by Date
problem_statement: Calculate running total of delivered revenue by date. Return order_date, daily_revenue, running_total.
hint: SUM() OVER(ORDER BY order_date ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING).
solution_sql: WITH daily AS (SELECT order_date, SUM(amount) AS daily_revenue FROM orders WHERE status='delivered' GROUP BY order_date) SELECT order_date, daily_revenue, SUM(daily_revenue) OVER(ORDER BY order_date) AS running_total FROM daily ORDER BY order_date

title: Top 3 Customers by Spend Per Region
problem_statement: Find top 3 customers by total spend in each region (delivered only). Return region, customer_name, total_spend, rank.
hint: Aggregate then RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_spend DESC).
solution_sql: WITH cs AS (SELECT c.region, c.name AS customer_name, SUM(o.amount) AS total_spend FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY c.region, c.name), rk AS (SELECT *, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY region ORDER BY total_spend DESC) AS rnk FROM cs) SELECT region, customer_name, total_spend, rnk FROM rk WHERE rnk<=3 ORDER BY region, rnk

title: Identify Late-Arriving Data
problem_statement: Find orders where delivery_date is more than 7 days after order_date. Return order_id, order_date, delivery_date, days_to_deliver.
hint: Use DATE_DIFF or date subtraction, filter > 7.
explanation: Late-arriving data detection for SLA monitoring.
solution_sql: SELECT order_id, order_date, delivery_date, delivery_date - order_date AS days_to_deliver FROM orders WHERE delivery_date IS NOT NULL AND (delivery_date - order_date) > 7 ORDER BY days_to_deliver DESC

title: Changed Records Between Source and Target
problem_statement: Find transactions where the amount differs between source and target system for the same txn_id. Return txn_id, source_amount, target_amount, difference.
hint: Self-join on txn_id filtering by system.
solution_sql: SELECT s.txn_id, s.amount AS source_amount, t.amount AS target_amount, t.amount - s.amount AS difference FROM transactions s JOIN transactions t ON s.txn_id = t.txn_id WHERE s.system = 'source' AND t.system = 'target' AND s.amount != t.amount

title: SCD Type 2 Current Active Rows
problem_statement: Get the current active record for each customer from the SCD table. Return customer_id, name, region, segment.
hint: Filter WHERE is_current = true.
explanation: SCD Type 2 basic — select current version.
solution_sql: SELECT customer_id, name, region, segment FROM scd_customers WHERE is_current = true ORDER BY customer_id

title: Rank Sellers by Revenue
problem_statement: Rank all sellers by their total delivered revenue using DENSE_RANK. Return seller_id, name, total_revenue, revenue_rank.
hint: Aggregate then DENSE_RANK() OVER(ORDER BY total_revenue DESC).
solution_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, s.name, SUM(o.amount) AS total_revenue, DENSE_RANK() OVER(ORDER BY SUM(o.amount) DESC) AS revenue_rank FROM sellers s JOIN orders o ON s.seller_id=o.seller_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.name ORDER BY revenue_rank

title: 7-Day Rolling Average Sales
problem_statement: Calculate 7-day rolling average of daily delivered revenue. Return order_date, daily_revenue, rolling_avg_7d.
hint: Use AVG() OVER(ORDER BY order_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW).
explanation: Rolling window — common in time-series analytics.
solution_sql: WITH daily AS (SELECT order_date, SUM(amount) AS daily_revenue FROM orders WHERE status='delivered' GROUP BY order_date) SELECT order_date, daily_revenue, ROUND(AVG(daily_revenue) OVER(ORDER BY order_date ROWS BETWEEN 6 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW), 2) AS rolling_avg_7d FROM daily ORDER BY order_date

title: Null Primary Key Detection
problem_statement: Find rows in orders where order_id, customer_id, or product_id is NULL. Return the full row.
hint: WHERE order_id IS NULL OR customer_id IS NULL OR product_id IS NULL.
explanation: Data quality check — NULL PKs break downstream.
solution_sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_id IS NULL OR customer_id IS NULL OR product_id IS NULL

title: Invalid Status Detection
problem_statement: Find orders with a status NOT in ('delivered','shipped','cancelled','returned'). Return order_id and status.
hint: WHERE status NOT IN (...).
solution_sql: SELECT order_id, status FROM orders WHERE status NOT IN ('delivered','shipped','cancelled','returned')

title: Unmatched Foreign Keys
problem_statement: Find orders referencing a customer_id that doesn't exist in the customers table. Return order_id and customer_id.
hint: LEFT JOIN orders to customers, WHERE customers.customer_id IS NULL.
solution_sql: SELECT o.order_id, o.customer_id FROM orders o LEFT JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE c.customer_id IS NULL

title: Reconcile Transaction Totals
problem_statement: For each account_id, compare total amounts between source and target systems. Return account_id, source_total, target_total, difference. Only show mismatched accounts.
hint: Aggregate by account_id and system, then pivot/join.
solution_sql: WITH src AS (SELECT account_id, SUM(amount) AS source_total FROM transactions WHERE system='source' GROUP BY account_id), tgt AS (SELECT account_id, SUM(amount) AS target_total FROM transactions WHERE system='target' GROUP BY account_id) SELECT COALESCE(s.account_id, t.account_id) AS account_id, COALESCE(s.source_total,0) AS source_total, COALESCE(t.target_total,0) AS target_total, COALESCE(t.target_total,0) - COALESCE(s.source_total,0) AS difference FROM src s FULL OUTER JOIN tgt t ON s.account_id=t.account_id WHERE COALESCE(s.source_total,0) != COALESCE(t.target_total,0) ORDER BY account_id

title: Employee Salary Above Department Average
problem_statement: Find employees earning above their department average. Return employee name, department_id, salary, dept_avg_salary.
hint: Use AVG() OVER(PARTITION BY department_id) and filter.
solution_sql: SELECT name, department_id, salary, ROUND(AVG(salary) OVER(PARTITION BY department_id), 2) AS dept_avg_salary FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(e2.salary) FROM employees e2 WHERE e2.department_id = employees.department_id) ORDER BY department_id, salary DESC

title: Month-over-Month Revenue Growth
problem_statement: Calculate month-over-month revenue growth for delivered orders. Return month, monthly_revenue, prev_month_revenue, growth_pct.
hint: Extract month, aggregate, then use LAG().
explanation: MoM growth — core business metric with LAG window function.
solution_sql: WITH monthly AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, SUM(amount) AS monthly_revenue FROM orders WHERE status='delivered' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date)) SELECT month, monthly_revenue, LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER(ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_revenue, ROUND(100.0*(monthly_revenue - LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER(ORDER BY month)) / LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER(ORDER BY month), 2) AS growth_pct FROM monthly ORDER BY month

title: Customer Lifetime Value
problem_statement: Compute CLV per customer: total spend, order count, avg order value, first and last order dates (delivered only). Return customer_id, name, total_spend, order_count, avg_order_value, first_order, last_order.
hint: Aggregate on customer with multiple aggregate functions.
solution_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name, SUM(o.amount) AS total_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count, ROUND(SUM(o.amount)/COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id),2) AS avg_order_value, MIN(o.order_date) AS first_order, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY total_spend DESC

title: Pipeline Failure Rate
problem_statement: Calculate the failure rate per pipeline. Return pipeline_name, total_runs, failed_runs, failure_rate_pct.
hint: GROUP BY pipeline and use conditional counts.
solution_sql: SELECT pipeline_name, COUNT(*) AS total_runs, SUM(CASE WHEN status='failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_runs, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN status='failed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 2) AS failure_rate_pct FROM pipeline_run_log GROUP BY pipeline_name ORDER BY failure_rate_pct DESC

title: Recon Mismatches
problem_statement: Find all source-target reconciliation mismatches. Return recon_date, table_name, source_count, target_count, count_diff.
hint: Filter status='mismatched' and compute diff.
solution_sql: SELECT recon_date, table_name, source_count, target_count, source_count - target_count AS count_diff FROM source_target_recon WHERE status = 'mismatched' ORDER BY recon_date

title: SCD2 History for a Customer
problem_statement: Show the full change history for customer_id=6 from the SCD table, ordered by effective_start. Return all columns.
hint: Simple filter + ORDER BY.
solution_sql: SELECT * FROM scd_customers WHERE customer_id=6 ORDER BY effective_start

title: SCD2 Point-in-Time Lookup
problem_statement: Find each customer's region as of 2022-06-01 from the SCD table. Return customer_id, name, region.
hint: Filter effective_start <= date AND effective_end > date.
explanation: Point-in-time query on SCD — important for historical analytics.
solution_sql: SELECT customer_id, name, region FROM scd_customers WHERE effective_start <= '2022-06-01' AND effective_end > '2022-06-01' ORDER BY customer_id

title: Inventory Depletion Rate
problem_statement: Calculate inventory depletion rate per product across snapshots. Return product_id, snapshot_date, qty_on_hand, prev_qty, depletion.
hint: Use LAG() to get previous snapshot and compute difference.
solution_sql: SELECT product_id, snapshot_date, qty_on_hand, LAG(qty_on_hand) OVER(PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY snapshot_date) AS prev_qty, LAG(qty_on_hand) OVER(PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY snapshot_date) - qty_on_hand AS depletion FROM inventory_snapshots ORDER BY product_id, snapshot_date

title: Missing Transactions in Target
problem_statement: Find txn_ids present in source but missing in target. Return txn_id, account_id, amount.
hint: Anti-join pattern between source and target subsets.
solution_sql: SELECT s.txn_id, s.account_id, s.amount FROM transactions s LEFT JOIN transactions t ON s.txn_id=t.txn_id AND t.system='target' WHERE s.system='source' AND t.txn_id IS NULL

title: Extra Records in Target
problem_statement: Find txn_ids present in target but not in source. Return txn_id, account_id, amount.
hint: Same as Q29 but reversed.
explanation: Detecting ghost records loaded into target.
solution_sql: SELECT t.txn_id, t.account_id, t.amount FROM transactions t LEFT JOIN transactions s ON t.txn_id=s.txn_id AND s.system='source' WHERE t.system='target' AND s.txn_id IS NULL

title: Customer Segment Revenue Share
problem_statement: Calculate each customer segment's share of total delivered revenue. Return segment, segment_revenue, total_revenue, share_pct.
hint: Two-level aggregation or window SUM for total.
solution_sql: WITH seg AS (SELECT c.segment, SUM(o.amount) AS segment_revenue FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY c.segment) SELECT segment, segment_revenue, SUM(segment_revenue) OVER() AS total_revenue, ROUND(100.0*segment_revenue/SUM(segment_revenue) OVER(), 2) AS share_pct FROM seg ORDER BY share_pct DESC

title: Order Status Distribution
problem_statement: Count orders by status. Return status, order_count, pct of total.
hint: GROUP BY status with window for total.
solution_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count, ROUND(100.0*COUNT(*)/SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS pct FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY order_count DESC

title: Employee Hierarchy Depth
problem_statement: Find each employee and their manager's name. Return employee_id, employee_name, manager_name (NULL if no manager).
hint: Self-join on manager_id = employee_id.
solution_sql: SELECT e.employee_id, e.name AS employee_name, m.name AS manager_name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id=m.employee_id ORDER BY e.employee_id

title: Category Revenue Ranking
problem_statement: Rank product categories by total delivered revenue. Return category, category_revenue, category_rank.
hint: JOIN, aggregate, RANK().
solution_sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(o.amount) AS category_revenue, RANK() OVER(ORDER BY SUM(o.amount) DESC) AS category_rank FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.product_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY category_rank

title: Pipeline Average Duration
problem_statement: Calculate the average run duration in minutes per pipeline (successful runs only). Return pipeline_name, avg_duration_minutes.
hint: Use EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM end_ts - start_ts)/60 or equivalent.
solution_sql: SELECT pipeline_name, ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (end_ts - start_ts))/60), 2) AS avg_duration_minutes FROM pipeline_run_log WHERE status='success' GROUP BY pipeline_name ORDER BY avg_duration_minutes DESC

title: Customers Active in All 3 Months
problem_statement: Find customers who placed at least one order in each of Jan, Feb, and Mar 2024. Return customer_id, name.
hint: COUNT(DISTINCT month) = 3 after extracting month.
solution_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE o.order_date >= '2024-01-01' AND o.order_date < '2024-04-01' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date)) = 3 ORDER BY c.customer_id

title: Order Amount Percentiles
problem_statement: Calculate the 25th, 50th (median), and 75th percentile of order amounts. Return p25, p50, p75.
hint: Use PERCENTILE_CONT or APPROX_QUANTILE.
solution_sql: SELECT ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY amount), 2) AS p25, ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY amount), 2) AS p50, ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY amount), 2) AS p75 FROM orders

title: Department Budget Utilization
problem_statement: Compare total employee salaries to department budget. Return dept name, total_salaries, budget, utilization_pct.
hint: JOIN and aggregate, divide salary sum by budget.
solution_sql: SELECT d.name AS dept_name, SUM(e.salary) AS total_salaries, d.budget, ROUND(100.0*SUM(e.salary)/d.budget, 2) AS utilization_pct FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id=d.department_id WHERE e.status='active' GROUP BY d.name, d.budget ORDER BY utilization_pct DESC

title: User Session Analysis
problem_statement: Count events per session and compute session duration in minutes. Return session_id, user_id, event_count, duration_minutes.
hint: GROUP BY session_id, use MIN/MAX timestamp diff.
solution_sql: SELECT session_id, user_id, COUNT(*) AS event_count, ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MAX(event_timestamp) - MIN(event_timestamp)))/60, 2) AS duration_minutes FROM events GROUP BY session_id, user_id ORDER BY duration_minutes DESC

title: Products Below Reorder Point
problem_statement: Using the latest inventory snapshot, find products where qty_on_hand < 100. Return product_id, warehouse_id, qty_on_hand.
hint: Filter for max snapshot_date then filter qty.
solution_sql: SELECT product_id, warehouse_id, qty_on_hand FROM inventory_snapshots WHERE snapshot_date = (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM inventory_snapshots) AND qty_on_hand < 100 ORDER BY qty_on_hand

title: Order Delivery SLA
problem_statement: Calculate avg delivery time in days per seller for delivered orders. Return seller_id, seller_name, avg_delivery_days.
hint: DATE_DIFF between delivery and order date.
solution_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, s.name AS seller_name, ROUND(AVG(o.delivery_date - o.order_date), 2) AS avg_delivery_days FROM orders o JOIN sellers s ON o.seller_id=s.seller_id WHERE o.status='delivered' AND o.delivery_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.name ORDER BY avg_delivery_days

title: Cancelled Order Rate by Seller
problem_statement: Calculate cancellation rate per seller. Return seller_id, name, total_orders, cancelled_orders, cancel_rate_pct.
hint: Conditional aggregation with CASE WHEN.
solution_sql: SELECT s.seller_id, s.name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.status='cancelled' THEN o.order_id END) AS cancelled_orders, ROUND(100.0*COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.status='cancelled' THEN o.order_id END)/COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id), 2) AS cancel_rate_pct FROM sellers s JOIN orders o ON s.seller_id=o.seller_id GROUP BY s.seller_id, s.name ORDER BY cancel_rate_pct DESC

title: Deduplicated Orders Table
problem_statement: Remove exact duplicate rows from orders. Return distinct rows sorted by order_id.
hint: SELECT DISTINCT * or ROW_NUMBER for dedup.
solution_sql: SELECT DISTINCT * FROM orders ORDER BY order_id

title: Event Funnel Analysis
problem_statement: Count unique users at each funnel stage: page_view > add_to_cart > purchase. Return event_type and user_count.
hint: Filter for funnel events and COUNT DISTINCT user_id.
solution_sql: SELECT event_type, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS user_count FROM events WHERE event_type IN ('page_view','add_to_cart','purchase') GROUP BY event_type ORDER BY CASE event_type WHEN 'page_view' THEN 1 WHEN 'add_to_cart' THEN 2 WHEN 'purchase' THEN 3 END

title: Inventory vs Sold Quantity
problem_statement: Compare latest inventory on hand vs total quantity sold (delivered). Return product_id, product_name, qty_on_hand, qty_sold, remaining.
hint: Join latest snapshot with aggregated sales.
solution_sql: WITH latest_inv AS (SELECT product_id, SUM(qty_on_hand) AS qty_on_hand FROM inventory_snapshots WHERE snapshot_date=(SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM inventory_snapshots) GROUP BY product_id), sold AS (SELECT product_id, SUM(quantity) AS qty_sold FROM orders WHERE status='delivered' GROUP BY product_id) SELECT p.product_id, p.name AS product_name, COALESCE(i.qty_on_hand,0) AS qty_on_hand, COALESCE(s.qty_sold,0) AS qty_sold, COALESCE(i.qty_on_hand,0)-COALESCE(s.qty_sold,0) AS remaining FROM products p LEFT JOIN latest_inv i ON p.product_id=i.product_id LEFT JOIN sold s ON p.product_id=s.product_id ORDER BY p.product_id
pyspark_equivalent: df computed with joins between latest inventory, sales aggregation, and products

title: SCD Type 1 Simulation
problem_statement: From scd_customers, build a Type 1 view: only the current record per customer, dropping history columns. Return customer_id, name, region, segment.
hint: Filter is_current=true, select relevant columns.
explanation: SCD1 only keeps latest values, no history.
solution_sql: SELECT customer_id, name, region, segment FROM scd_customers WHERE is_current = true ORDER BY customer_id

title: Consecutive Pipeline Failures
problem_statement: Find pipelines with 2 or more consecutive failures. Return pipeline_name, run_id, status, prev_status.
hint: LAG() to get previous status, filter both='failed'.
explanation: Alert on repeated failures — ops monitoring.
solution_sql: WITH lagged AS (SELECT pipeline_name, run_id, status, LAG(status) OVER(PARTITION BY pipeline_name ORDER BY start_ts) AS prev_status FROM pipeline_run_log) SELECT pipeline_name, run_id, status, prev_status FROM lagged WHERE status='failed' AND prev_status='failed'

title: Revenue by Day of Week
problem_statement: Calculate total delivered revenue by day of week. Return day_of_week (name) and revenue.
hint: Use DAYNAME or EXTRACT(DOW).
solution_sql: SELECT DAYNAME(order_date) AS day_of_week, SUM(amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status='delivered' GROUP BY DAYNAME(order_date) ORDER BY revenue DESC

title: Customer Order Gap Analysis
problem_statement: For each customer, calculate the days between consecutive orders. Return customer_id, order_date, prev_order_date, gap_days.
hint: LAG(order_date) OVER(PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date).
explanation: Inter-event timing — useful for churn prediction.
solution_sql: SELECT customer_id, order_date, LAG(order_date) OVER(PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date) AS prev_order_date, order_date - LAG(order_date) OVER(PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date) AS gap_days FROM orders ORDER BY customer_id, order_date

title: DENSE_RANK Products by Category Revenue
problem_statement: Rank products within each category by delivered revenue using DENSE_RANK. Return category, product_name, product_revenue, product_rank.
hint: Aggregate then DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY category).
solution_sql: SELECT p.category, p.name AS product_name, SUM(o.amount) AS product_revenue, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY p.category ORDER BY SUM(o.amount) DESC) AS product_rank FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.product_id WHERE o.status='delivered' GROUP BY p.category, p.name ORDER BY p.category, product_rank

title: Multi-Table Join: Full Order Details
problem_statement: Create a full order detail view joining orders, customers, sellers, and products. Return order_id, customer_name, seller_name, product_name, amount, status. Show first 20 rows.
hint: Four-way JOIN.
explanation: Multi-table joins — common in dimensional modeling.
solution_sql: SELECT DISTINCT o.order_id, c.name AS customer_name, s.name AS seller_name, p.name AS product_name, o.amount, o.status FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id JOIN sellers s ON o.seller_id=s.seller_id JOIN products p ON o.product_id=p.product_id ORDER BY o.order_id LIMIT 20

title: Year-over-Year Comparison Scaffold
problem_statement: Calculate monthly revenue for Q1 2024 (Jan-Mar). Return month_num, month_name, monthly_revenue.
hint: Extract month, filter Q1, aggregate.
explanation: Monthly aggregation — building block for YoY.
solution_sql: SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) AS month_num, MONTHNAME(order_date) AS month_name, SUM(amount) AS monthly_revenue FROM orders WHERE status='delivered' AND order_date >= '2024-01-01' AND order_date < '2024-04-01' GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date), MONTHNAME(order_date) ORDER BY month_num

title: Cross-Seller Customer Overlap
problem_statement: Find customers who have purchased from more than one seller. Return customer_id, name, seller_count.
hint: COUNT(DISTINCT seller_id) > 1.
solution_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.seller_id) AS seller_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT o.seller_id)>1 ORDER BY seller_count DESC
